food_subgroup: Carbonated soft drink with ice cream (soda float)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Sprite Float; FOODON_03301737 (ice cream soda) is the closest parent category covering carbonated beverage with ice cream.

preparation_method: ice cream scooped into carbonated beverage

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, consisting of roughly 250 ml of Sprite and one scoop (approximately 100 g) of vanilla ice cream.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate: USDA FoodData Central — Carbonated beverage, lemon-lime (NDB 14148) and Vanilla ice cream (NDB 19095)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Sprite Float is a composite beverage consisting of approximately 71% Sprite (lemon-lime carbonated soft drink) and 29% vanilla ice cream by weight in a 350 g serving. Per-100g values are calculated as a weighted blend: 71 g Sprite (USDA NDB 14148: 41 kcal, 0g protein, 0g fat, 10.6g carb, 10.6g sugar, 0g fibre, 7mg sodium per 100g) and 29 g vanilla ice cream (USDA NDB 19095: 207 kcal, 3.5g protein, 11g fat, 6.3g sat fat, 23.6g carb, 21.4g sugar, 0.7g fibre, 80mg sodium per 100g). Weighted blend per 100g of float: energy ~89 kcal, protein ~1.0g, fat ~3.2g, sat fat ~1.8g, carb ~14.4g, sugar ~14.3g, fibre ~0.2g, sodium ~28mg.

gi_source.title: Analogous estimate: Ice cream (GI ~51) and lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (GI ~59); composite weighted GI estimated at 55 for Sprite Float
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sprite Float. GI estimated as a weighted composite: Sprite (lemon-lime carbonated soft drink) has a GI of approximately 59 (University of Sydney GI Database, regular carbonated soft drink reference); vanilla ice cream has a GI of approximately 51 (University of Sydney GI Database, full-fat vanilla ice cream). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: Sprite contributes ~75% of carbohydrate load (10.6g × 0.71 = 7.5g) and ice cream ~25% (23.6g × 0.29 = 6.8g). Weighted GI = (7.5 × 59 + 6.8 × 51) / (7.5 + 6.8) = (442.5 + 346.8) / 14.3 = 55.2, rounded to 55. gi_category set to low (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per 350g serving = (55 × 50.4g carb) / 100 = 27.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~28 mg per 100g; ~98 mg per 350g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Sprite Float has a low sodium content and is unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure when consumed occasionally, but the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain which is an indirect risk factor for hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~50g per 350g serving) from both Sprite and ice cream, with moderate glycemic index of 55 and glycemic load of 27.7 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Sprite Float due to its high free sugar content and substantial glycemic load per serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ice cream (~6.3g per 350g serving) and high free sugar content contributing to dyslipidaemia and weight gain risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sprite Float should be consumed sparingly by individuals with cardiovascular disease, as the combination of saturated fat from ice cream and high free sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and contribute to excess caloric intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sprite Float is a popular cold beverage dessert drink consisting of chilled Sprite lemon-lime carbonated soft drink with one or more scoops of vanilla ice cream added directly into the glass. As the ice cream melts into the fizzy soda, it creates a creamy, frothy, sweet-tart beverage with a light effervescent texture. The drink is widely served at casual dining restaurants, hawker-style eateries, and fast-food outlets across Singapore and Southeast Asia, including at Indian-cuisine establishments such as Baba Eating House. It is typically served in a tall glass and consumed as a dessert beverage or sweet accompaniment to a meal. Nutritionally, a standard 350g serving provides approximately 312 kcal, with the energy derived primarily from free sugars (~50g) contributed by both the Sprite and the ice cream. The fat content (~11g per serving) comes entirely from the dairy ice cream component and includes approximately 6.3g of saturated fat. Protein content is modest (~3.5g per serving). The glycemic load per serving is high at approximately 27.7, making this drink a significant source of rapidly absorbed carbohydrates. Clinically, Sprite Float is not recommended for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or obesity due to its high free sugar and caloric density. It is also a concern for cardiovascular health due to saturated fat from ice cream. The drink contains dairy allergens and is not suitable for vegans or lactose-intolerant individuals.

confidence_reason: Sprite Float is a composite beverage assembled from two commercially standardised components (Sprite and vanilla ice cream). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for each component using a defensible weight-based blend ratio. No direct HPB Singapore or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific drink. GI is an analogous composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for each component. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact ice cream brand and scoop size served at Baba Eating House are unknown.
notes: Sprite Float is a composite beverage; per-100g nutrient values are calculated as a weighted blend of approximately 71% Sprite (USDA NDB 14148) and 29% vanilla ice cream (USDA NDB 19095) by weight in a standard 350g serving. Vitamin C is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite in the source database. Vitamin B1 (thiamine) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin B2 (riboflavin) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin B3 (niacin) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin B6 is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin B12 is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Folate is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. The cuisine tag in the input JSON is listed as Indian because the serving restaurant (Baba Eating House) is an Indian-cuisine establishment; however, Sprite Float itself is an internationally generic beverage and has been reclassified accordingly. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish conventions as this food is served in a Singapore context. GI set to 55 (low category, GI ≤ 55) based on weighted composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values. Glycemic load per 350g serving = (55 × 50.4g total carbohydrate) / 100 = 27.7.
